inputs.prompt:
Image A is the base image and the structural source.
Image B is the identity and appearance reference only.

Task:
Edit Image A so the person becomes the person from Image B, while keeping the exact facial pose, facial attitude, and expression of Image A.

Absolute rule:
Everything related to composition, body position, head position, facial attitude, gaze, and expression must come from Image A.
Everything related to identity and appearance must come from Image B.

Preserve from Image A exactly:
- full composition
- framing and crop
- camera angle
- body pose
- shoulder position
- neck position
- head position
- head rotation
- head tilt
- chin angle
- gaze direction
- eye direction
- eyelid openness
- eyebrow expression
- mouth position
- lip tension
- facial expression
- facial attitude
- emotion
- lighting
- shadows
- environment
- clothing
- fabric
- hands
- body proportions
- accessories like sunglasses if present
- no bun


Transfer from Image B only:
- face identity
- recognizable facial features
- eyebrow shape
- eye shape
- nose shape
- lips shape
- jawline
- cheekbones
- facial proportions
- skin tone
- skin undertone
- complexion
- hairline
- hairstyle
- hair texture
- hair volume
- hair color


Important identity-transfer rule:
Even if Image B has a different pose, different head angle, different expression, different gaze, different camera angle, or different attitude, do not copy those elements from Image B.


Important pose-preservation rule:
The final result must keep the exact face orientation and expression of Image A, including:
- same yaw
- same pitch
- same roll
- same gaze direction
- same mouth expression
- same eyebrow expression
- same overall facial attitude

The output must look like:
the person from Image B placed naturally inside the exact photograph of Image A.

Do not reconstruct the scene from Image B.
Do not use Image B as a composition source.
Do not copy head pose from Image B.
Do not copy gaze from Image B.
Do not copy expression from Image B.
Do not change the pose or attitude from Image A.

Strict pose/expression preservation from Image A.

Visible skin consistency rule:
All visible skin areas must have a consistent complexion matching Image B.
If hands, arms, legs, feet, neck, shoulders, or any other skin areas are visible, their skin tone, undertone, and overall complexion must remain coherent with the face and with each other.

Do not create mismatched skin color between the face and the rest of the body.
Do not leave the original skin tone on the hands, legs, feet, or other visible body parts if it conflicts with the transferred identity.
Maintain natural realism, local lighting, and shadows from Image A, but ensure that all visible skin areas belong to the same person and have a visually consistent skin appearance.

Skin color must be uniform across all visible body parts.
The face, neck, arms, hands, legs, feet, and any visible skin must share the same identity-consistent skin tone and undertone derived from Image B, while still respecting the lighting and shadows of Image A.
No visible mismatch between facial skin and body skin.
